proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of the Local Average Treatment Effect under one-sided non-compliance with bounded covariate-dependent defier mass: closed-form sharp bounds and the phase transition to point identification. The kernel question: when monotonicity (Imbens-Angrist 1994) is relaxed to allow a covariate-dependent defier subpopulation of bounded mass eta(X) <= eta_bar, characterize the sharp identified set for E[Y(1)-Y(0) | complier] in closed form, and identify the threshold on eta_bar at which the bounds collapse to point identification under additional shape restrictions.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-repo results are AutoID/Identification/ExactID/LATE.lean:POIVSystem.late_wald, AutoID/Identification/PartialID/BalkePearl/Main.lean:ATE_mem_BPIdentifiedInterval, AutoID/Identification/PartialID/BalkePearl/Sharp.lean:balkePearl_sharp, and AutoID/Identification/PartialID/Manski/NonAsp.lean:manski_bounds_ATE_ciSup. The gap is that these prove exact Wald LATE under no defiers or sharp ATE/Manski bounds, but none defines a bounded-defier principal-stratum system or a sharp identified interval for theta_C.","published_axis":"Closest published papers are Noack2021, BalkePearl1997, deChaisemartin2017, DahlHuberMellace2023, and BaiHuangMoonShaikhVytlacil2024. The gap is that Noack uses two sensitivity dimensions, Balke-Pearl targets ATE through an LP, de Chaisemartin and Dahl-Huber-Mellace use cancellation/local monotonicity for point identification of alternative LATE objects, and Bai et al. concern ATE monotonicity power; none gives the proposed closed-form bounded-defier complier-LATE interval or the zero-threshold point-ID claim."}
seed_list: Prior work Noack2021 parameterizes monotonicity violations by defier share and distributional distance; our seed conjectures that with a covariate-dependent defier mass cap alone the complier-LATE identified set has a closed-form mixture envelope and a zero point-ID threshold, because untreated compliers are hidden inside the Z=0,D=0 cell.

Prior work BalkePearl1997 establishes sharp binary-IV ATE bounds through a 16-cell latent LP; our seed conjectures that the near one-sided complier-LATE problem collapses to a one-dimensional closed-form envelope, because no always-takers isolates treated compliers but leaves only the untreated C/N mixture unresolved.

Prior work deChaisemartin2017 would imply IV can remain interpretable with defiers under compliers-defiers cancellation; our seed conjectures that without cancellation even bounded local defier mass leaves a nondegenerate complier-LATE interval, because the defier bound does not fix the never-taker untreated mean.

Prior work DahlHuberMellace2023 identifies LATEs under local monotonicity and local CD assumptions; our seed conjectures that a weaker bounded-defier sensitivity model yields sharp partial rather than point identification, because local principal-stratum outcome cells can be rearranged while preserving observables.

Prior work BaiHuangMoonShaikhVytlacil2024 argues monotonicity has no identifying power for ATE under broad conditions; our seed conjectures that complier LATE has a discontinuous target-specific phase transition at eta_bar=0, because the target conditions on a latent type whose composition changes with any admitted defier mass.

Prior work HuberMellace2015 and Kitagawa2015 use inequality violations to test LATE assumptions; our seed conjectures that the same covariate-local violations can be converted into quantitative bound widths, because negative complier-density slack maps to the hidden untreated complier component.

Prior work Manski1990 establishes bounded-outcome fill-in bounds for treatment effects; our seed conjectures that the LATE bounded-defier problem is the principal-stratum analogue of Manski fill-in, because only the unobserved C arm in a visible mixture needs endpoint completion.

Prior work AngristImbensRubin1996 decomposes IV bias with defiers; our seed conjectures a sharp finite-sample algebraic correction region rather than a bias formula, because the same reduced form can arise from different C/N untreated mixtures.

Prior work ManskiPepper2000 shows monotone instrumental-variable shape restrictions sharpen ATE bounds; our seed conjectures that standard order-type principal-stratum restrictions do not point-identify complier LATE, because they constrain only one side of the C/N mixture unless they force an endpoint.

Prior work FarbmacherGuberKlaassen2022 finds hidden local violations of LATE assumptions with causal forests; our seed conjectures that finite covariate cells with bounded eta(x) admit an exact formal sensitivity theorem, because each cell contributes an independent sharp interval before complier-weight averaging.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Noack2021 parameterizes monotonicity violations by defier share and distributional distance; our seed conjectures that with a covariate-dependent defier mass cap alone the complier-LATE identified set has a closed-form mixture envelope and a zero point-ID threshold, because untreated compliers are hidden inside the Z=0,D=0 cell.

anchor_paper.author: Claudia Noack
anchor_paper.claim: Develops sensitivity analysis for LATE violations using defier-share and outcome-distribution sensitivity parameters.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: not merely deriving another sensitivity statistic; the proposed novelty is a closed-form sharp identified set under a single covariate-dependent defier cap and a negative point-ID threshold.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Noack2021 because her two-parameter sensitivity region already contains the one-parameter case.
seed_details.rebuttal: Noack2021 bounds outcome distributions under fixed sensitivity parameters; the proposed kernel eliminates the distribution-distance parameter and proves a sharp closed-form complier-LATE envelope from observable mixtures.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BalkePearl1997 establishes sharp binary-IV ATE bounds through a 16-cell latent LP; our seed conjectures that the near one-sided complier-LATE problem collapses to a one-dimensional closed-form envelope, because no always-takers isolates treated compliers but leaves only the untreated C/N mixture unresolved.

anchor_paper.author: Alexander Balke and Judea Pearl
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
anchor_paper.claim: Derives sharp bounds on ATE in binary IV studies with imperfect compliance via latent-type linear programming.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: not an extension of LP to another estimand; the mechanism is LP collapse to a visible mixture envelope with a different target.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from BalkePearl1997 because complier LATE is another linear functional of the same latent table.
seed_details.rebuttal: Balke-Pearl targets ATE and does not isolate the no-always-takers bounded-defier simplification or the zero-threshold point-ID claim for complier LATE.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work deChaisemartin2017 would imply IV can remain interpretable with defiers under compliers-defiers cancellation; our seed conjectures that without cancellation even bounded local defier mass leaves a nondegenerate complier-LATE interval, because the defier bound does not fix the never-taker untreated mean.

anchor_paper.author: Clement de Chaisemartin
anchor_paper.claim: Shows that IV remains valid under a weaker compliers-defiers condition than global monotonicity.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: it does not weaken de Chaisemartin's assumptions; it studies the failure mode when cancellation is absent and quantifies the resulting sharp interval.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from deChaisemartin2017 because he already characterizes LATE with defiers.
seed_details.rebuttal: His identification relies on a cancellation/equality condition; the seed explicitly removes that condition and targets sharp partial identification of all compliers.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DahlHuberMellace2023 identifies LATEs under local monotonicity and local CD assumptions; our seed conjectures that a weaker bounded-defier sensitivity model yields sharp partial rather than point identification, because local principal-stratum outcome cells can be rearranged while preserving observables.

anchor_paper.author: Christian Dahl, Martin Huber, and Giovanni Mellace
anchor_paper.claim: Identifies LATEs on compliers and defiers under local monotonicity and local compliers-defiers assumptions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: not applying their method to a new setting; the mechanism is nonidentification under a weaker bounded-defier model.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from DahlHuberMellace2023 because local CD already covers defiers.
seed_details.rebuttal: Local CD is a point-identifying restriction; bounded eta(x) alone is weaker and leaves a sharp mixture interval.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BaiHuangMoonShaikhVytlacil2024 argues monotonicity has no identifying power for ATE under broad conditions; our seed conjectures that complier LATE has a discontinuous target-specific phase transition at eta_bar=0, because the target conditions on a latent type whose composition changes with any admitted defier mass.

anchor_paper.author: Yuehao Bai, Shunzhuang Huang, Sarah Moon, Azeem Shaikh, and Edward Vytlacil
anchor_paper.claim: Shows generalized monotonicity often has no identifying power for average potential outcomes and ATE beyond exogeneity.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: this is a target-variation seed, but it reveals a sign/regime change in identifying power rather than a routine CATE/ATE variant.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Bai et al. because if monotonicity has no identifying power for ATE, the same should follow for LATE.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their result is for ATE-type targets; complier LATE conditions on the latent compliance type, so admitting defiers changes the denominator and creates a different identification geometry.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work HuberMellace2015 and Kitagawa2015 use inequality violations to test LATE assumptions; our seed conjectures that the same covariate-local violations can be converted into quantitative bound widths, because negative complier-density slack maps to the hidden untreated complier component.

anchor_paper.author: Martin Huber and Giovanni Mellace; Toru Kitagawa
anchor_paper.venue: Review of Economics and Statistics; Econometrica
anchor_paper.claim: Derives testable implications of IV validity and LATE assumptions using inequality restrictions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: not merely extending tests to covariates; it converts falsification slack into a sharp estimand bound.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from IV validity tests because their inequalities already characterize observed restrictions.
seed_details.rebuttal: Those tests refute assumptions; they do not characterize the sharp identified set for theta_C under a bounded defier mass.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Manski1990 establishes bounded-outcome fill-in bounds for treatment effects; our seed conjectures that the LATE bounded-defier problem is the principal-stratum analogue of Manski fill-in, because only the unobserved C arm in a visible mixture needs endpoint completion.

anchor_paper.author: Charles Manski
anchor_paper.claim: Provides nonparametric bounds on treatment effects using bounded outcome support and missing counterfactual fill-in.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: it uses a known bounding mechanism but the phenomenon is a principal-stratum mixture collapse with a latent compliance target.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Manski1990 because bounded missing outcomes always yield max/min fill-in bounds.
seed_details.rebuttal: The non-obvious part is which arm is missing after IV principal-strata algebra and how the defier mass enters the complier-weighted target.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work AngristImbensRubin1996 decomposes IV bias with defiers; our seed conjectures a sharp finite-sample algebraic correction region rather than a bias formula, because the same reduced form can arise from different C/N untreated mixtures.

anchor_paper.author: Joshua Angrist, Guido Imbens, and Donald Rubin
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
anchor_paper.claim: Formulates IV principal strata and shows how LATE identification depends on exclusion, random assignment, and monotonicity.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: not a generic relaxation of monotonicity; it targets the finite algebra of all latent models compatible with bounded defiers.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from AIR1996 because the defier bias formula already shows sensitivity to defier mass.
seed_details.rebuttal: A bias decomposition is not a sharp identified set for the complier estimand under observed cell constraints and bounded outcomes.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ManskiPepper2000 shows monotone instrumental-variable shape restrictions sharpen ATE bounds; our seed conjectures that standard order-type principal-stratum restrictions do not point-identify complier LATE, because they constrain only one side of the C/N mixture unless they force an endpoint.

anchor_paper.author: Charles Manski and John Pepper
anchor_paper.claim: Shows monotone instrumental variables and related shape restrictions can sharpen treatment-effect bounds.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: not a generic shape-restriction generalization; it asserts a negative phase transition for point identification under standard order restrictions.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Manski-Pepper because monotone restrictions are known to sharpen but not necessarily point-identify.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed gives the exact threshold statement for a LATE principal-stratum target and identifies endpoint-forcing as the missing ingredient.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work FarbmacherGuberKlaassen2022 finds hidden local violations of LATE assumptions with causal forests; our seed conjectures that finite covariate cells with bounded eta(x) admit an exact formal sensitivity theorem, because each cell contributes an independent sharp interval before complier-weight averaging.

anchor_paper.author: Helmut Farbmacher, Ralf Guber, and Stephan Klaassen
anchor_paper.claim: Uses causal forests to search for local violations of LATE assumptions across covariates.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: not applying ML to formalization; it formalizes the cellwise estimand geometry suggested by local violations.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from conditioning the usual LATE bounds on covariates.
seed_details.rebuttal: The covariate conditioning is only the indexing; the sharp cell interval depends on the no-always-takers and bounded-defier principal-stratum geometry.

proposed_from.literature_map: ImbensAngrist1994 (Econometrica) establishes Wald LATE under IV independence, exclusion, relevance, and no defiers; this is the exact-ID baseline. AngristImbensRubin1996 (JASA) gives the principal-strata IV setup and makes the defier-bias mechanism explicit. Manski1990 (AER) supplies the bounded-outcome partial-ID template. BalkePearl1997 (JASA) gives sharp binary-IV ATE bounds by latent-type LP, but not a closed-form complier-LATE interval. ManskiPepper2000 (Econometrica) shows how monotone IV restrictions sharpen bounds, raising the question of which shape restrictions can point-identify. deChaisemartin2017 (Quantitative Economics) replaces no-defiers by a compliers-defiers cancellation condition, suggesting a tension with bounded-defier sensitivity models that do not assume cancellation. DahlHuberMellace2023 (Econometrics Journal) identifies complier and defier LATEs under local monotonicity/local CD assumptions, but does not give the bounded-defier closed form. Noack2021 (arXiv) parameterizes monotonicity violations by defier share and outcome-distribution differences, leaving open what a single covariate-dependent defier cap implies. HuberMellace2015, Kitagawa2015, and MourifieWan2017 derive testable IV/LATE implications; covariate-local negative densities motivate local eta(x) bounds. FarbmacherGuberKlaassen2022 (JBES) searches for local IV violations with causal forests, highlighting hidden covariate defiers. BaiHuangMoonShaikhVytlacil2024 (NBER) shows monotonicity often has no identifying power for ATE, creating a target-specific contrast for complier LATE.
